qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Codex 输出归位 docs/codex/ — 你明示要求；终结所有 subagent 的无 tee 绕路

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Hook file contains changes referencing task 20260521-090200 (sentinel check extension). These changes are from a different in-flight cycle, not this cycle (20260521-090100). Not a finding for this cycle.
Hook file contains Write-overwrite sentinel grant consume logic added by task 20260522-080646-B. Different in-flight cycle. Not a finding for 20260521-090100.

Step 2 captures printed path into $CODEX_OUT for optional Read step but explicitly warns NOT to pass $CODEX_OUT as tee argument. The _CODEX_USE_CANONICAL variable is set in Step 2 bash block; Steps 3/4 re-declare the same condition inline. This means _CODEX_USE_CANONICAL must persist across bash invocations or the inline condition must be re-evaluated. The inline tee targets make this safe — each tee is guarded by its own ${_CODEX_USE_CANONICAL:-0} check, defaulting to fallback if unset.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: policies/tool-policy.v1.json now has */docs/codex/ in allowed_write_path_prefixes for all 11 consultation roles. commands/codex.md and /root/.agents/skills/codex/SKILL.md both use hook-resolvable $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt tee targets with no opaque $CODEX_OUT variable as tee argument. Subagent tee workarounds are terminated.

complaint_element_pointer.how_located: File-level verification via policy JSON + markdown file inspection

qa.overall_assessment: All 6 acceptance criteria pass. Exactly 11 roles (ba, qa, pm, architect, product-owner, user, ui-specialist, spec, prompt-inspector, cleanliness-inspector, style-inspector) have */docs/codex/ in allowed_write_path_prefixes. policy_version=4. commands/codex.md and SKILL.md Step 2 bash blocks are byte-identical with ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$ guard, explicit ./.. rejection, two-level canonical path, mkdir-p, and fallback. All 6 tee invocations (3 per file) use only hook-resolvable forms. No TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels remain. No regressions. No band-aid patterns.

qa._success_criteria_rule: 3 requirements decomposed into 6 ACs. All 6 ACs verified via pytest + direct file inspection.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause was two-part: (1) docs/codex/ missing from policy — fixed: 11 roles now have */docs/codex/ in allowed_write_path_prefixes. (2) tee '$CODEX_OUT' form blocked by _resolve_path() — fixed: tee targets now use $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/codex/$CODEX_OUT_TASK_ID/$CODEX_OUT_ROLE.txt which _resolve_path() expands via $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR prefix. Both parts confirmed addressed. No hook files were modified (constraints satisfied).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex Skill entered the same self-referential loop documented in the dev report: treated the QA prompt as $ARGUMENTS and ran its own step-dispatch instructions, reproducing the codex.md document body rather than invoking the Codex CLI binary. This is a known systemic issue (dev report codex_consult.status: 'failed_parse').
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted per graceful fallback protocol. Self-review covered: (1) over-engineering — no, branch structure is minimum required to satisfy AC4 (each physical tee must be hook-resolvable inline); (2) shell variable scope — _CODEX_USE_CANONICAL defaults to 0 safely via ${_CODEX_USE_CANONICAL:-0}; (3) regression risk — all 11 target roles retain existing */var/tmp/codex-outputs/ prefix; non-target roles unchanged; hook files unchanged; (4) scope drift — SKILL.md outside repo is documented and not a violation; hook file diffs are from other cycles; (5) /close debate readiness — all 6 tests pass with real assertions; no TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels; Step 2 bash blocks byte-identical; writer-vs-reader path consistent (Step 5 reads 'path printed in Step 2').
